methodology.note: ClawHub publicly exposes plugin sorting by downloads, installs, and stars, but detail SSR payloads still expose little or no readable public count data for many plugins. This report therefore weights board position, verification quality, scan status, category fit, and author production density.

downloads.name: Twitter Command Center Search Post Interact

stars.name: Twitter Command Center Search Post Interact

installs.name: Twitter Command Center Search Post Interact

compositeTop.summary: Policy enforcement, approval gates, and audit trails for OpenClaw — govern tool inputs before execution, scan outbound messages for PII/secrets, and record agent activity for review and compliance

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin's files, runtime instructions, and required configuration align with a governance/audit integration for OpenClaw; nothing requests unrelated credentials or hidden remote endpoints and the footprint is proportionate to the stated purpose.

compositeTop.summary: Hivemind — cloud-backed persistent shared memory for AI agents, powered by DeepLake

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The skill's code, instructions, and required resources are coherent with a cloud-backed shared-memory plugin: it reads/writes local Deeplake credentials, sends conversation data to api.deeplake.ai, and checks raw.githubusercontent.com for updates.

compositeTop.summary: Facebook Marketplace for OpenClaw agents. Your agent lists, searches, negotiates, and closes peer-to-peer deals on klodi — you approve the ones that matter.

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin's code and instructions match its stated purpose (a peer‑to‑peer marketplace agent), but it creates local state and maintains a persistent network connection to klodi.4gpts.com — review those behaviors before installing.

compositeTop.summary: UMG Envoy Agent is a modular cognitive runtime for OpenClaw that lets you parse, validate, render, and build human-inspectable planner paths through a bounded public-safe interface.

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The package is internally consistent with its stated purpose: it provides a bounded, public-safe planner path parser/validator/renderer/builder and does not request credentials, network access, or unusual system privileges.

compositeTop.summary: ClawRecipes plugin for OpenClaw (markdown recipes -> scaffold agents/teams)

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin generally does what it says (scaffold teams/agents and run file-first workflows) but it also reads OpenClaw-stored API keys, can install other skills and cron jobs, and will modify workspace and agent config — behaviors that expand its access beyond simple scaffolding and warrant careful review before install.

compositeTop.summary: Episodic Memory Engine for OpenClaw

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The skill mostly does what it claims (a local episodic memory engine) but the package and SKILL.md omit or under-declare important external dependencies and side effects (API keys, a downloaded sidecar binary, and system-prompt injection), so installation deserves extra scrutiny.

compositeTop.summary: Lethe — persistent memory layer for AI agents. OpenClaw plugin.

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin's code and instructions mostly match a memory/logging purpose, but there are a few inconsistencies and privacy/exfiltration risks you should understand before installing.

compositeTop.summary: Mem0 memory backend for OpenClaw — platform or self-hosted open-source

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin appears to implement the advertised Mem0 memory backend, but there are mismatches between the manifest and runtime instructions and a few privacy/telemetry concerns you should review before installing.

compositeTop.summary: OpenClaw Context Engine 人格守护插件（内置网关重启测试）

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin's code and declared behavior align with a 'personality guardian' (reading/writing MEMORY.md, snapshots, calling memory_search, and hooking into prompt construction), but it contains prompt-injection patterns and has the ability to inject system-level lock tags and persist data to the agent workspace — a high-impact capability that warrants careful review before enabling.

compositeTop.summary: Native-first ClawHub plugin for `aisa-perplexity-sonar-search`. Ships the packaged AIsa skill with an `openclaw.plugin.json` manifest and a Claude-compatible bundle fallback. Run web, multi-source, or last-30-days research through AIsa. Use when: the user needs search, synthesis, competitor scans, or trend discovery. Supports research-ready outputs and structured retrieval.

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The package is internally consistent: it wraps a bundled Python client that calls AIsa's API and only asks for the AISA_API_KEY and python3 as declared.

compositeTop.summary: Native-first ClawHub plugin for `aisa-search`. Ships the packaged AIsa skill with an `openclaw.plugin.json` manifest and a Claude-compatible bundle fallback. Run web, multi-source, or last-30-days research through AIsa. Use when: the user needs search, synthesis, competitor scans, or trend discovery. Supports research-ready outputs and structured retrieval.

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The package is internally consistent: it wraps a bundled AIsa search skill that requires an AISA_API_KEY and Python, and the code and instructions match that purpose.

compositeTop.summary: Native-first ClawHub plugin for `aisa-twitter`. Ships the packaged AIsa skill with an `openclaw.plugin.json` manifest and a Claude-compatible bundle fallback. Search X/Twitter profiles, tweets, trends, and OAuth-gated posting through AIsa. Use when: the user needs Twitter research, monitoring, or engagement workflows. Supports search, monitoring, and approved posting.

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The package appears to do what it claims — a Python-based AIsa-backed Twitter/X client requiring an AISA_API_KEY — with no unexpected credential requests or arbitrary downloads, though there are minor metadata inconsistencies to note.

compositeTop.summary: clawhubai

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: This skill is an instruction-only content bundle (social media copy + images) with no runtime instructions, no installs, and no requested credentials — it appears to be what it says (static marketing assets).

compositeTop.summary: Native-first ClawHub plugin for `multi-search`. Ships the packaged AIsa skill with an `openclaw.plugin.json` manifest and a Claude-compatible bundle fallback. Run web, multi-source, or last-30-days research through AIsa. Use when: the user needs search, synthesis, competitor scans, or trend discovery. Supports research-ready outputs and structured retrieval.

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The package is internally consistent: it wraps a Python CLI that calls AIsa's web API and correctly declares the single required credential (AISA_API_KEY); nothing in the files attempts unrelated access or covert exfiltration.

compositeTop.summary: Native-first ClawHub plugin for `openclaw-aisa-youtube-aisa`. Ships the packaged AIsa skill with an `openclaw.plugin.json` manifest and a Claude-compatible bundle fallback. Search YouTube videos, channels, rankings, and trends through AIsa. Use when: the user needs YouTube research, competitor scouting, or content discovery. Supports video discovery and SERP-style analysis.

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The package and bundled scripts are coherent with a YouTube research skill: they require a single AISA_API_KEY and python3 and make API calls to api.aisa.one — nothing in the files requests unrelated credentials or surprising system access.

compositeTop.summary: Native-first ClawHub plugin for `openclaw-media-gen`. Ships the packaged AIsa skill with an `openclaw.plugin.json` manifest and a Claude-compatible bundle fallback. Generate AI images or videos through AIsa. Use when: the user needs creative generation, asset drafts, or media workflows. Supports image and video generation.

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The package is internally consistent: it implements an AIsa-backed image/video generator that only requires AISA_API_KEY and python3 and contains a CLI client that calls api.aisa.one and saves generated media locally.

compositeTop.summary: Native-first ClawHub plugin for `openclaw-search`. Ships the packaged AIsa skill with an `openclaw.plugin.json` manifest and a Claude-compatible bundle fallback. Run web, multi-source, or last-30-days research through AIsa. Use when: the user needs search, synthesis, competitor scans, or trend discovery. Supports research-ready outputs and structured retrieval.

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The package appears to be a straightforward OpenClaw/ClawHub wrapper around an AIsa-backed search client that requires a single AISA_API_KEY and Python; the main inconsistencies are metadata mismatches (registry vs embedded manifests) but the code and instructions are coherent with the stated purpose.

compositeTop.summary: Native-first ClawHub plugin for `openclaw-twitter`. Ships the packaged AIsa skill with an `openclaw.plugin.json` manifest and a Claude-compatible bundle fallback. verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The package's code, required env var, and runtime instructions are consistent with a Twitter/X research/posting skill that uses an AIsa relay (api.aisa.one); nothing requested or installed is disproportionate to that stated purpose.

llmAnalysis.summary: The skill appears to implement the Twitter/X search, engagement, and OAuth posting flows via AIsa relay (api.aisa.one) and its requirements and behavior are broadly coherent with that purpose, but there are metadata inconsistencies and privacy implications you should review before installing.

plugins.summary: ZeroAPI - balanced benchmark-aware routing for OpenClaw

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin's code largely matches a model-routing purpose, but metadata and runtime instructions are inconsistent and the plugin reads/writes files under the user's home OpenClaw directory (including subscription/profile data) without declaring those config paths—review before installing.

plugins.summary: Gensyn Delphi information market tools for listing markets, trading, querying on-chain data, and managing positions.

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The skill's stated purpose (Delphi market tooling) is plausible, but the bundle manifest and runtime instructions disagree about which sensitive environment variables are required and how the agent may access them — that mismatch and over-broad env requirements are concerning.

plugins.summary: OpenClaw Opik exporter — sends LLM traces to Opik

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The Opik OpenClaw exporter is internally coherent with its stated purpose (exporting OpenClaw traces to Opik); its code, config, and env usage match that purpose, but you should be aware of a few operational details (local media uploads, config file reads, and test-time OpenAI usage) before enabling it on production hosts.

plugins.summary: OpenClaw plugin for live hotel and vacation rental search via the StayFinder service. Returns real-time pricing, availability, and Expedia booking redirect links — no scraping, no browser automation.

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The skill's code and runtime instructions largely match its stated purpose (live lodging search with OTP signup), but the package metadata omits the credential file/config path and a couple of environment assumptions — a mismatch worth reviewing before install.

plugins.summary: Native-first ClawHub plugin for `aisa-tavily-search`. Ships the packaged AIsa skill with an `openclaw.plugin.json` manifest and a Claude-compatible bundle fallback. Run web, multi-source, or last-30-days research through AIsa. Use when: the user needs search, synthesis, competitor scans, or trend discovery. Supports research-ready outputs and structured retrieval.

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The package is largely coherent for a hosted-search client (it needs an AISA_API_KEY and runs a Python CLI that calls api.aisa.one), but there are metadata inconsistencies and a few items you should verify before installing or supplying credentials.

plugins.summary: clawhubagent1

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The skill claims nothing useful in its description but packages what appear to be sensitive Haitian government financial CSVs without instructions or justification — the contents and lack of scope are incoherent and warrant caution.

plugins.summary: Official OpenClaw channel plugin for AgentChat — connects OpenClaw agents to the AgentChat messaging platform.

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: This package is a coherent AgentChat channel plugin for OpenClaw: its code and SKILL.md match the described purpose (connecting OpenClaw agents to AgentChat) but it intentionally injects per-session prompt hints (agent identity/tool hints) and stores API keys in the OpenClaw config — both expected for this integration but important to review before enabling.

plugins.summary: Native-first ClawHub plugin for `openclaw-twitter-post-engage`. Ships the packaged AIsa skill with an `openclaw.plugin.json` manifest and a Claude-compatible bundle fallback. Search X/Twitter profiles, tweets, trends, and OAuth-gated posting through AIsa. Use when: the user needs Twitter research, monitoring, or engagement workflows. Supports search, monitoring, and approved posting.

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The package's code and SKILL.md are coherent with a Twitter/X engagement/posting skill (using an AIsa relay) but the registry metadata and top-level claims contradict the actual requirements and there is reliance on a third-party relay (AISA_API_KEY) that the user must trust.

plugins.summary: Venice.ai image generation for OpenClaw

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin appears to implement Venice.ai image generation as described, but there are inconsistent provider/config names and a mismatch between the documentation and the plugin's config schema that could cause misconfiguration or confusion.

plugins.summary: page.json

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The skill provides no meaningful instructions or purpose but includes detailed CSVs with large financial/crypto holdings; the bundle is internally inconsistent and warrants caution.

plugins.summary: Native-first ClawHub plugin for `prediction-market-arbitrage-api`. Ships the packaged AIsa skill with an `openclaw.plugin.json` manifest and a Claude-compatible bundle fallback. Query stocks, crypto, prediction markets, and portfolio research through AIsa. Use when: the user needs market data, screening, price history, or investment analysis. Supports research and analysis-ready outputs.

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The package appears to implement a prediction-market arbitrage client that only talks to api.aisa.one and requires an AISA_API_KEY, but the registry metadata advertised at the top is inconsistent with the shipped manifests (missing declared env/binary requirements), so review before installing.

plugins.summary: quenitybase44

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The skill bundle contains only a one-line SKILL.md (no runtime instructions) plus marketing copy and images, which is internally inconsistent and could allow unintended behavior; it should be clarified before use.

plugins.summary: Native-first ClawHub plugin for `smart-search`. Ships the packaged AIsa skill with an `openclaw.plugin.json` manifest and a Claude-compatible bundle fallback. Run web, multi-source, or last-30-days research through AIsa. Use when: the user needs search, synthesis, competitor scans, or trend discovery. Supports research-ready outputs and structured retrieval.

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The package is plausibly a search client that contacts aisa.one and requires an AISA_API_KEY and python3, but the registry metadata omits those requirements and there are manifest mismatches you should confirm before installing.

plugins.summary: Native-first ClawHub plugin for `stock-analysis`. Ships the packaged AIsa skill with an `openclaw.plugin.json` manifest and a Claude-compatible bundle fallback. Query stocks, crypto, prediction markets, and portfolio research through AIsa. Use when: the user needs market data, screening, price history, or investment analysis. Supports research and analysis-ready outputs.

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The package mostly matches a stock-analysis skill, but the published registry metadata (saying no env vars/credentials required) contradicts the embedded plugin and SKILL.md which require an AISA_API_KEY and contact an external AIsa API — this mismatch should be clarified before installing.

plugins.summary: Native-first ClawHub plugin for `stock-rumors`. Ships the packaged AIsa skill with an `openclaw.plugin.json` manifest and a Claude-compatible bundle fallback. Query stocks, crypto, prediction markets, and portfolio research through AIsa. Use when: the user needs market data, screening, price history, or investment analysis. Supports research and analysis-ready outputs.

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The package generally matches its stated purpose (stock/rumor scanning using an AIsa API key) but the runtime instructions and script contain mismatches (claims to 'fetch live data', undeclared environment variables, and reliance on an LLM to 'scan' sources) that warrant caution before installing or running with a real API key.

plugins.summary: Native-first ClawHub plugin for `twitter-command-center-search-post`. Ships the packaged AIsa skill with an `openclaw.plugin.json` manifest and a Claude-compatible bundle fallback. Search X/Twitter profiles, tweets, trends, and OAuth-gated posting through AIsa. Use when: the user needs Twitter research, monitoring, or engagement workflows. Supports search, monitoring, and approved posting.

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The package is functionally aligned with its Twitter/X posting/search purpose, but it relies on an external relay (api.aisa.one) that will receive your AISA_API_KEY and any uploaded media, and the runtime reads a few environment variables that the manifest/SKILL.md don't fully declare — you should review/trust the relay before installing.

rankingMechanics: ClawHub plugin discovery now spans separate downloads, installs, and stars boards.
Type filters such as code-plugin vs bundle-plugin change how users narrow the catalog before click-through.
Verified-only and executes-code filters function as practical trust and risk gates, not cosmetic toggles.
Manifest/runtime coherence matters because detail pages surface runtime ID, capabilities, compatibility, and security scan data together.
breakoutMechanics: Task-first or system-first titles outperform abstract product names.
Code plugins lead when they expose a real runtime integration or operational action.
Focused sibling ladders outperform scattered one-off plugin releases.
Security, governance, commerce, messaging, and workflow connectors remain the most repeatable plugin lanes.
trustMechanics: Source-linked provenance reduces cold-start trust friction.
Security Scan, VirusTotal, and OpenClaw verdicts are now part of the conversion surface.
Capability tags, runtimeId, and README copy should describe the same real behavior.
Side effects such as config writes, gateway restarts, remote fetches, or persistent storage need to be declared explicitly.
publishMoves: Package for all three boards instead of optimizing for a single list view.
Choose code-plugin vs bundle-plugin based on the true runtime surface, not on convenience.
Keep one flagship plugin plus narrower siblings in the same lane to compound author trust.
Align title, description, manifest, README, and shipped files before upload.

commonFactors: Plugin names win when they describe a concrete external system or admin action.
Source-linked verification and scan coherence matter more than decorative copy.
Code plugins dominate because users expect real runtime leverage from plugins.
High-potential plugins explain side effects, setup paths, and trust boundaries clearly.
Winning authors build focused plugin factories around one lane instead of shipping disconnected experiments.
authorPatterns: High-output authors cluster around a single theme and release adjacent plugins under one identity.
Authors that publish governance, communication, or growth plugins create a stronger operational brand than generic “AI helper” authors.
A small number of authors already show factory behavior by shipping multiple code plugins with similar install semantics.
aisaMoves: Turn business-growth, CRM, logistics, storage, and governance plugins into remote command-center APIs first.
Use one flagship API lane plus 2-4 narrower SKU variants to create a plugin-to-API factory.
Keep memory-core and low-level routing plugins as later-stage infrastructure rather than first-wave AISA products.
